comments.comment: Increase in water loadings due to variation of annual sampling results

type.en: Reason why substance was added to the report
type.fr: Raison pour laquelle la substance a été ajoutée au rapport

comments.comment: Increase in water loadings due to variation of annual sampling results

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The Mining Association of Canada conducted a comparative review of reported NPRI emissions from various mining facilities across Canada.  This review indicated that the reported particulate emissions for Teck Coal were high relative to other operations.  Teck Coal performed a detailed review of their emission calculations to identify areas of conservatism with the intention of refining the calculated values to reflect releases more accurately.  As a result of this review, the following changes were made, which resulted in significant decreases in the quantity of fugitive dust reported for the 2023 reporting year.  This is understood to represent a more accurate picture of emissions at Teck Coal operations.

Previous estimates for fugitive dust were calculated using site specific values for silt and moisture content for coal and waste rock.  As a result of the detailed review conducted for the 2023 reporting year, it was determined that the site-specific silt content does not represent the proper size fraction range for use with U.S. EPA AP-42 calculations.  Therefore, the U.S. EPA AP-42 typical mean values for silt and moisture content for western surface coal mining were used for coal and waste rock.  Emissions from reclamation dozer activity decreased due to the change in silt content.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

reasonsForNoActivities.en: Activities were implemented in a previous year; additional activities are either unnecessary or unfeasible at this time
reasonsForNoActivities.fr: Des activités ont été mises en oeuvre lors d'une année précédente; des activités supplémentaires sont soit inutiles ou irréalisables actuellement

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The main driver in the decrease in particulate emissions from CRM is a change in the stockpile sources.  Since CRM is closed and in maintenance mode, outdoor stockpile areas were considered inactive, and emissions were calculated based on emission factors from AP-42 chapter 11.9, for wind erosion of exposed areas, as opposed to previous years which considered emissions based on active stockpiles. Decreased dozer hours , distance travelled by vehicles on unpaved road, and annual average wind speed, as well as an increased number of rainy days and snow cover in 2022 also helped reduce particulate releases.
